title: Sales CRM - how to manage different customer types that need different columns
excerpt: I’m quite new to monday and still working out how to customise our sales crm.  We deal with 2 different customer types: Producers & Resellers.  In the Leads board, I want the customer type/status to trigger different set…

posts.author: Juls11 (Julie Elmes)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m quite new to monday and still working out how to customise our sales crm. We deal with 2 different customer types: Producers & Resellers. In the Leads board, I want the customer type/status to trigger different sets of questions (that will show in the mobile app). So, if the customer is a Producer, the producer questions show but not the Reseller questions. Can I do this on the same Leads board or do I need separate boards? Thanks.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Julie,

Are you looking to trigger different sets of questions in a form you’re using via the board? I ask this, as it sounds like our conditional questions feature would be useful for you here, but I want to make sure I am understanding the format correctly. Unless you’re using a form, it isn’t natively supported for questions/columns to appear/disappear based on a specific selection, so perhaps 2 seperate boards is the way to go. I look forward to hearing from you!

posts.author: Juls11 (Julie Elmes)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Bianca. I think I’ve worked a way around it.

posts.author: LuckyStrike (LuckyStrike)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve used item cards and separate views for each customer type - makes it cleaner, and i don’t overcrowd one board with too many columns that aren’t relevant to anyone

posts.author: brahosatad1974 (Brooke)
posts.cookedHtml: I ran into the same problem trying to handle clients with very different needs. What helped me was separating them into different boards and syncing shared fields where needed. For deeper customer insights and smoother follow-ups, I’ve started using Acumatica Cloud ERP Customer Management | AcuPower alongside monday—it lets me keep track of customer activity and communication without cluttering my boards with too many custom fields.
